raw_consumer_statement:
on XX/XX/XXXX purchased a tennis bracelet of XXXX posted to my bank account on XX/XX/XXXX. I did not take possession of it until XX/XX/XXXX because of some sizing they had to do. So 34 days since purchased had already past before I was able to take possession. THE very same day, on XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX hours after the XXXX XXXX employee put it on me it fell off with no provocation. I had XXXX witnesses that saw it just fall off my wrist. A friend of mine placed back on assuming it had not been latched on properly the first time ; it only lasted a few more hours then fell off again. On XX/XX/XXXX I texted the jeweler and let her know that it had fallen off my wrist XXXX and now it is lost. She said if i found it to bring it and get it fixed. When i finally found it i took it in and they kept it another XXXX to repair it.... So at this point it 94 days since it had been in XXXX 's XXXX and 29 days that it was lost due to the defectiveness of the product. in several texts the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX would refuse to refund me as i urged her it was a defective bracelet. 
she only offered to repair. 

on XX/XX/XXXX they asked me to come pick it up and i took a picture of the item list that detailed all the " repairs '' they did to it. Soon there after not 24 hours later it fell off my wrist again and again I was not able to find it for 25 days.!!! When i found it I submitted a dispute on XX/XX/XXXX, on my charge card with FROST ; and on XX/XX/XXXX, I was told that 120 days had passed and they had denied my claim due to coming forward after the 120 days had passed. The merchant kept the bracelet 94 days of the 120 days and the defective product was lost for 55 days. Due to these circumstances the fault for the 120 day limit was out of my control.
